scope_note: QA RT Quadrance Orbit Divisibility cert [283]. Primary source: Wildberger, N. J. (2005). Divine Proportions: Rational Trigonometry to Universal Geometry. Wild Egg Books. ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8. Chapter 1 defines quadrance Q(A,B) = (x2-x1)^2 + (y2-y1)^2. For QA direction vector (b,e), quadrance G = b^2+e^2 (raw, not mod-reduced). Mechanism: QA Orbit Access Theorem cert [279]; orbit classifier qa_orbit_rules.orbit_family on (b, e, 9). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): for (b,e) in {1,...,9}^2, the 3-adic valuation of the quadrance G = b^2+e^2 is determined exactly by the orbit family: Cosmos -> v3(G)=0 (G coprime to 3); Satellite -> v3(G)=2 (G=9k, gcd(k,3)=1); Singularity -> v3(G)=4 (G=162=2*81). Equivalently: v3(G) = 2*v3(gcd(b,e)) for all 81 pairs in {1,...,9}^2. Verified exhaustively. Corollary (scope boundary): the spread s=(b1*e2-b2*e1)^2/(G1*G2) between any two QA directions is invariant under 3-adic scaling of (b,e) — the orbit-class 3-adic factors in G cancel completely in the spread numerator (Lagrange identity). Therefore the spread denominator in lowest terms carries NO information about orbit class; the orbit-class information lives in G itself. Cert does NOT certify spread denominators (they are orbit-class-invariant by the scale argument above). Cert does NOT claim G values outside {1,...,9}^2. Cert does NOT claim the v3(G) result extends to mod-24 QA. Theorem NT compliance: G = b^2+e^2 is integer arithmetic; v3(G) and gcd(b,e) are integer operations; no float feedback into QA layer. Lineage: discovered 2026-05-30 while formulating the Wildberger RT denominator domain sweep claim; the spread denominator claim was refined away (scale-invariance); the quadrance claim survived as a clean exhaustive certification.
